title: MR or CT Enterography - Crohn’s Disease
description: This MR or CT enterography structured report template was created by members of the Society of Abdominal Radiology Crohn's disease-focused panel. Instructions for using this template are included throughout the document. For additional instructions, hover the mouse over the Crohn's disease related sections in the "Findings" section. The purpose of this template is to provide a framework for reporting MR and CT enterography in patients with Crohn’s disease. This template should be modified to comply with individual institutional requirements.
author: rsna_00005112
templateData:
Procedure Information


MR Enterography
CT Enterography


Contrast Enhanced MR Enterography
Contrast Enhanced CT Enterography

Tesla

Clinical Information

Comparison

Findings
There is no bowel wall thickening, abnormal bowel wall enhancement, or evidence of obstruction. (INSTRUCTIONS: If there are abnormal bowel segments, delete this sentence.)
_________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
INSTRUCTIONS FOR CREATING A REPORT FOR ABNORMAL BOWEL SEGMENTS:
Combine sections 1 - 8 below to create sentences describing the findings for bowel segments in each disease category (i.e. "active inflammation without luminal narrowing" or "stricture with active inflammation"). If there is more than 1 abnormal bowel segment with the same disease category, list the findings in detail, the location and length of the most severely involved segment and provide the number of similar abnormal bowel segments. Repeat for each bowel segment that has a different disease category. Alternatively, if there is more than one abnormal bowel segment with the same disease category, each abnormal segment can be described in detail, rather than just the most severely abnormal segment. After describing the abnormal bowel segments, compare to prior studies. If there are no abnormal bowel segments, delete sections 1 - 8 below.
_________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
SAMPLE REPORT 1:
There is moderate wall thickening with stratified hyperenhancement, intramural edema, restricted diffusion and luminal narrowing involving a 12 cm long segment of the distal and terminal ileum consistent with active inflammation with luminal narrowing.
_________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
SAMPLE REPORT 2:
There are multiple (5) segments of active inflammation without luminal narrowing in the jejunum and ileum. The most severe segment is in the mid ileum measuring 4 cm in length and shows asymmetric mural hyperenhancement, mild wall thickening, intramural edema and restricted diffusion. These segments are improved compared to the prior study.
_________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
SAMPLE REPORT 3:
There are multiple (3) segments of stricture with active inflammation in the ileum. The most severe segment is in the distal ileum measuring 5 cm in length and shows stratified mural hyperenhancement, moderate wall thickening, mild upstream dilation, intramural edema, restricted diffusion and decreased motility and has an associated enterocolic fistula to the sigmoid colon. These segments are without significant change compare to the prior study.

Perianal fistula (if possible, report fistulas using the Parks' or St. James' classification system)
Perianal abscess (size of abscess in cm)

Impression

_________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
INSTRUCTIONS FOR CREATING IMPRESSION STATEMENTS:
Crohn’s disease MRE and CTE reports can include up to 6 impression statements as indicated below. For less complex cases, impression statements 1 - 3 can be combined into one or two impression statement(s). Impression statements should be modified to comply with individual institutional requirements.
1. INFLAMMATION STATEMENT: If bowel inflammation is present, note the site, length and severity. Potential impression statements include, “No imaging signs of active inflammation”, “Nonspecific small bowel inflammation”, “Active inflammatory small bowel Crohn’s Disease without luminal narrowing”, “Active inflammatory small bowel Crohn’s Disease with luminal narrowing”, or “Crohn’s Disease with no imaging signs of active inflammation”. If inflammation is present, compare to prior studies to evaluate for disease response or progression.
2. STRICTURE STATEMENT: If a stricture is present, describe the site, length and degree of upstream dilation. Potential impression statements include, “Stricture with imaging findings of active inflammation” or “Stricture without imaging findings of active inflammation”. If upstream bowel dilation is moderate to severe, consider adding the term, “…with small bowel obstruction”.
3. PENETRATING DISEASE STATEMENT: Describe the type of penetrating disease that is present and if associated with a stricture and/or enteric inflammation. Potential impression statements include sinus tract, fistula (simple or complex), inflammatory mass, abscess or free perforation.
4. PERIANAL CROHN’S DISEASE STATEMENT: State if a perianal fistula or abscess is present. If a fistula is present, state if this is simple or complex. If possible, perianal fistulas should be reported using the Parks’ or St. James’ classification system.
5. OTHER COMPLICATIONS STATEMENT: State if extraintestinal complications related to Crohn’s disease (or Crohn’s disease treatment) is present including femoral head avascular necrosis, sacroiliitis, primary sclerosing cholangitis, pancreatitis, mesenteric venous thrombosis, chronic mesenteric venous occlusion, neoplasm, cholelithiasis, or nephrolithiasis.
6. OTHER SIGNIFICANT FINDINGS STATEMENT: State if other significant findings unrelated to Crohn’s disease are present such as hepatic steatosis, a pancreatic mass or indeterminate ovarian mass.
_________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
SAMPLE REPORT 1:
1. No evidence of small bowel inflammation.
2. No perianal fistula or abscess.
_________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
SAMPLE REPORT 2:
1. Active inflammation with luminal narrowing involving the distal and terminal ileum.
2. Hepatic steatosis.
_________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
SAMPLE REPORT 3:
1. Multiple segments of active inflammation with luminal narrowing in the ileum, overall improved compared to (date of prior study).
2. Multiple segments of stricture with active inflammation in the jejunum and ileum, with an associated enterocolic fistula between the distal ileum and sigmoid colon, overall without significant change compared to (date of prior study).
3. No perianal fistula or abscess.
4. Bilateral sacroiliitis.
_________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
SAMPLE REPORT 4:
1. Three ileal strictures with superimposed active inflammation associated with penetrating disease. The strictures are discontiguous and measure 3 cm, 5 cm and 5 cm. The most proximal stricture is associated with small bowel obstruction.
2. Penetrating disease involving the distal ileal stricture with a complex fistula that includes two enteroenteric fistulas and an enterocolic and enterovesical fistula. There is no abscess.
3. Right posterior intersphincteric perianal fistula extending to the inferomedial gluteal fold.
4. Left femoral head avascular necrosis.
